disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Multiple similar pigmented lesions present on the trunk for several years, increasing in number with advancing age
trigger_question_examples: Do you have any other spots or moles like this one?
Is this the only spot, or do you have several similar ones?
Have you noticed more of these spots appearing over the years?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Oh yeah, I've got heaps of these dark spots on my back and chest. They've been there for years and I seem to keep getting more of them as I get older.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The bleeding began immediately after mechanical trauma — the lesion was caught and partially torn by a towel — rather than starting spontaneously
trigger_question_examples: Did the bleeding start on its own, or after the spot was knocked or caught on something?
What exactly were you doing when it started bleeding?
Was there any injury or friction to the spot before it bled?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It only started bleeding after I caught it with the towel and it tore. It wasn't doing anything on its own before that — I basically ripped part of it off by accident.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The lesion has a long-standing warty, rough, 'stuck-on' surface and had been asymptomatic apart from occasional friction irritation
trigger_question_examples: Can you describe the surface of the spot — is it smooth, rough, warty, crusty?
Does the spot feel raised and sit on top of the skin, or is it flat?
How long has it looked the way it does, and has the texture changed?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's a raised, rough, warty-looking thing that sort of sits on top of the skin — almost like it's stuck on. It's looked like that for years and never hurt, it just sometimes rubbed on my clothes.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Patient takes regular daily low-dose aspirin — a contributor to the bleeding
trigger_question_examples: Are you taking any blood-thinning medicines, like aspirin or warfarin?
What medications do you take regularly?
Do you take anything that could make you bleed or bruise more easily?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I take a low-dose aspirin every day, one tablet. I've been on it for a while.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Severe health anxiety focused specifically on skin cancer / melanoma
trigger_question_examples: What are you most worried this might be?
Is there something specific you're afraid of here?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Honestly, I'm terrified this is a melanoma. I've been panicking about skin cancer ever since it tore and started bleeding.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I'm just really scared, that's all.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Before the trauma the lesion had not spontaneously changed in size, shape or colour, and no other lesions are ulcerating or bleeding on their own (melanoma change-history screen — negative)
trigger_question_examples: Before it caught on the towel, had the spot changed in size, shape, or colour on its own?
Has this spot or any of your others been growing, changing colour, or bleeding without being knocked?
Had you noticed anything different about it in the weeks before this happened?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, it hadn't changed on its own — it's been the same for years until I caught it. And none of my other spots are bleeding or changing.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I hadn't really been watching it closely.
